title: aconitum lycoctonum subsp. puberulum
intro: Aconitum lycoctonum subsp. puberulum is a subspecies of the Aconitum lycoctonum plant, also known as Monk's Hood or Bearflower, which is a perennial flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. The plant contains aconitine, a toxic alkaloid that has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Aconitum lycoctonum subsp. puberulum have been used to treat various health conditions, including pain relief, fever reduction, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried, powdered, or made into a tincture or ointment, which can be used topically or internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
